---
status: public
shortDescription: "To test whether an expression is true or false, one must compare it to true or to false"
shortCorrection: "To test whether an expression is true or false, one can just use it"
concepts:
  - Boolean
  - Expression
  - Operator
  - Equality
related:
  - id: MapToBooleanWithConditionalOperator
    relation: Similar detour (via conditional operator)
  - id: MapToBooleanWithIf
    relation: Similar detour (via if statement)
  - id: NoAtomicExpression
    relation: Is a variable name alone an expression
  - id: ReturnCall
    relation: When used in return statement

  - id: jls13
    sections: ["15.21.2","15","4.2.5"]

# from: jls8

- id: ExpressionAsTree
  how: expression involving comparison of boolean subexpression to boolean literal
text:
  - id: ofj6
    sections: ["2"]
---

To determine whether an expression evaluates to `true` or `false`,
one must use a relational operator (`==` or `!=`)
to compare with a boolean literal (`true` or `false`).

frontmatter.shortDescription: To test whether an expression is true or false, one must compare it to true or to false
frontmatter.shortCorrection: To test whether an expression is true or false, one can just use it
